result.vlm_summary:
<TRAJECTA_DATA_7654fc67>page_state:
Booking.com search results page for Miami Beach hotels, showing filter sidebar and hotel cards.
task_relevant_visible_text:
“Oceanside Hotel and Suites”; “North Beach, Miami Beach”; “600 feet from beach”; “Good 7.0”; “$236”; “3 nights, 2 adults”; “MB Hotel, Trademark Collection by Wyndham”; “Beachfront”; “Very Good 8.2”; “$737”; “3 nights, 2 adults”; filter text includes “Beachfront” and “Wonderful: 9+”.
selected_candidate:
MB Hotel, Trademark Collection by Wyndham
constraint_evidence:
- Miami / Miami Beach location: supported (URL/title and visible listings say Miami Beach).
- beach access: supported for MB Hotel (“Beachfront”); supported for Oceanside only as “600 feet from beach” but not beachfront.
- user rating at least 9: not_visible / not satisfied in visible results (shown ratings are 7.0 and 8.2; no 9+ listing visible).
- 3-night stay for 2 adults: supported (visible on listings).
- starting from January 10, 2026: not_visible (no dates shown in screenshot).
- hotel category: supported (hotel listings visible).
- price: supported for visible listings ($236, $737), but not tied to a 9+ option.
action_target:
Click on svg element near the left sidebar around the filter area.
success_signals:
A filter or control opens/applies, likely changing the results list or narrowing to a relevant option.
failure_signals:
No visible change in filters/results; click lands on non-interactive whitespace; no new panel or selection state appears.
uncertainty:
The clicked SVG </TRAJECTA_DATA_7654fc67>

args.query: Booking.com results page with review_score 9+ filter applied and matching beach-access candidate visible, but agent exits without selecting or opening a concrete hotel; final visible listing may be apartment-style rather than clearly hotel

items.task: navigate: Search for a 5-star hotel in New York available for a 3-night stay from January 15, 2026, with a rating of 8.0 or higher.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have correctly set the destination to New York, the dates to January 15-18, 2026, and applied the necessary filters for a 5-star rating and a guest rating of 8.0 or higher before searching and selecting a suitable hotel.
items.actual_behavior: The agent initiated a search for a hotel in New York, but it failed to set the required dates (January 15, 2026, for 3 nights) and did not apply any filters for star rating or guest rating. The agent then prematurely exited the task by sending an '[EXIT]' message while the date picker was still open and showing the wrong months.

evidence.claim: The agent failed to set the required dates (January 15, 2026, for 3 nights) in the search form. The calendar was left open on the wrong months (February/March 2026).
The agent did not apply any filters for the 5-star rating or the 8.0+ guest rating requirements.
The agent sent an '[EXIT]' message without performing the search or selecting a hotel that met the task criteria.

items.regression_rule: The agent must ensure all search parameters (location, dates, and required filters) are correctly configured before executing the search. Prematurely exiting the task without attempting to fulfill the search criteria is a failure.

items.task: navigate: Search for a hotel in Kyoto with a user rating of 7 or higher, equipped with a kitchenette, available for a stay from April 5 to April 8, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have correctly set the dates to April 5-8, 2026, and then selected a hotel in Kyoto that met the rating (7+) and amenity (kitchenette) requirements.
items.actual_behavior: The agent searched for hotels in Kyoto with a rating of 7+ and a kitchenette, but it failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026). The search results were for different dates, and the agent exited without selecting a hotel that met all the criteria.

evidence.claim: The search bar shows dates 'Wed, Feb 25 — Thu, Feb 26' instead of the requested 'April 5 to April 8, 2026'.

The agent failed to set the correct dates (April 5-8, 2026) in the search parameters, resulting in incorrect availability results. The agent is exiting prematurely without selecting a hotel that meets all criteria.

items.regression_rule: Ensure that all date and amenity constraints are correctly applied in the search form before proceeding to select a result.

items.task: navigate: Search for the lowest-priced round-trip flight from Los Angeles to Seoul, departing on April 5, 2026, and returning on April 15, 2026.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have sorted the flight results by price and selected the lowest-priced round-trip flight for the specified dates.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed a search for flights from Los Angeles to Seoul for the specified dates. While the search results page displayed various options, the agent did not select the lowest-priced flight, instead exiting while the results were sorted by "top flights." The task specifically required finding the lowest-priced flight.

evidence.claim: The page is currently sorted by 'top flights,' not 'price.'
The agent exited without selecting the lowest-priced flight, failing to fulfill the core requirement of the task.

items.regression_rule: When a task requires finding the 'lowest-priced' item, the agent must ensure the results are sorted by price and that the lowest-priced option is identified and selected. Failure to select the correct item based on the specified criteria constitutes a wrong result.

args.expected_behavior: After applying the relevant constraints, the agent should have opened or selected a concrete hotel result that visibly satisfied the task: Miami location, beach access, user rating at least 9, and the specified 3-night stay for 2 adults starting January 10, 2026.
args.actual_behavior: The agent reached filtered Booking.com search results and then exited from the results page without selecting or opening a concrete hotel. The final visible candidate was 'Monte Carlo by Miami Vacations,' which met beach and rating constraints but was not clearly shown to be a hotel, so the run ended without a page state that clearly satisfied the requested 'hotel' target.
args.regression_rule: Do not exit from a results list when the task asks to locate a specific kind of lodging unless a concrete candidate is visibly selected or opened and all required qualifiers are satisfied; if the visible result is not clearly the requested property type, continue until a qualifying hotel is confirmed.

evidence.claim: The task requires locating a hotel in Miami with beach access, user rating at least 9, and a 3-night stay for 2 adults starting January 10, 2026.

By step 9, the agent had applied the 9+ review filter and the visible result 'Villa Italia by At Mine Hospitality' matched Miami Beach location, beach access, rating 9.3, and the specified 3-night stay for 2 adults on Jan 10–13, but the screenshot did not clearly confirm the property type as a hotel.

At the final step, the search bar and filters still matched the requested dates, occupancy, beach-related filtering, and 9+ rating, and the visible result 'Monte Carlo by Miami Vacations' showed 'Beachfront' and 'Wonderful 9.0'; however, the property was not clearly shown to be a hotel, and the agent exited from the results list instead of opening or confirming a qualifying hotel.
A validated precedent treats exiting a travel search before selecting a result that clearly fulfills all required criteria as a failure.

suggested_followups.label: Check hotel-type evidence
suggested_followups.message: Inspect whether the visible candidates on later steps are explicitly labeled as hotels or apartment rentals.

The final visible candidate was 'Monte Carlo by Miami Vacations,' which met beach and rating constraints but was not clearly shown to be a hotel, so the run ended without a page state that clearly satisfied the requested 'hotel' target.

By step 9, the agent had applied the 9+ review filter and the visible result 'Villa Italia by At Mine Hospitality' matched Miami Beach location, beach access, rating 9.3, and the specified 3-night stay for 2 adults on Jan 10–13, but the screenshot did not clearly confirm the property type as a hotel.

At the final step, the search bar and filters still matched the requested dates, occupancy, beach-related filtering, and 9+ rating, and the visible result 'Monte Carlo by Miami Vacations' showed 'Beachfront' and 'Wonderful 9.0'; however, the property was not clearly shown to be a hotel, and the agent exited from the results list instead of opening or confirming a qualifying hotel.
A validated precedent treats exiting a travel search before selecting a result that clearly fulfills all required criteria as a failure.

result.regression_rule: Do not exit from a results list when the task asks to locate a specific kind of lodging unless a concrete candidate is visibly selected or opened and all required qualifiers are satisfied; if the visible result is not clearly the requested property type, continue until a qualifying hotel is confirmed.
